I’d be happy to help with that! Based on the contract titled "Abbott Laboratories: MSA - Dec 09 - SYMPHONY", here’s what Symphony granted the client under Section 2 regarding license rights:
Symphony grants Abbott Laboratories a non-exclusive, non-sublicensable, non-transferable license to use the Software (called Synoma®) during the agreement term.
The license is strictly for converting certain personal information elements into Tokens as described in the implementation documentation.
Use of the Software is limited to the specified location and must be used as provided, including any updates or modifications from Symphony or its trusted third parties.
Abbott may allow its IT support contractors access only if they need it, are bound by similar confidentiality terms, and Abbott remains responsible for their actions.
Symphony may provide updates, bug fixes, or enhancements, which Abbott must implement within 60 days.
Abbott is prohibited from copying, modifying, selling, distributing, reverse engineering, or creating derivative works of the Software.
Symphony retains exclusive ownership of all rights and intellectual property in the Software and any modifications.
Abbott must keep the Software confidential, restrict access, and notify Symphony immediately if confidentiality is compromised.
Symphony has the right to audit Abbott’s compliance with these terms with prior notice.
If you want, I can provide more details or help with other sections of the contract!

final.actual_answer:
I found detailed information about the data protection and privacy provisions in Amendment 1 of the contract titled "Century Therapeutics November 2023 MSA." Here's a summary focusing on the Data Protection Agreement (DPA), Data Privacy Exhibit, and related safeguards:
Amendment Overview:
Amendment 1, effective January 6, 2025, modifies the original MSA dated November 30, 2023.
It specifically updates the data protection processing provisions.
Data Protection Exhibit Update:
Section (2) of Exhibit B - Data Protection is replaced to ensure compliance with Chapter V of the GDPR.
The Parties agree to sign the EU Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) modules one and four to govern international transfers of personal data related to the services.
New Annexes Added:
Annex 2: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
Annex 3: Technical and Organisational Measures (TOMs) These annexes are incorporated into Exhibit B - Data Protection.
Key Data Protection Safeguards:
The Parties commit to implementing appropriate technical and organizational measures to secure personal data, including during transmission, to prevent accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure, or access.
Security measures include encryption or pseudonymisation where feasible.
The data exporter assists the data importer in ensuring security and must notify the importer promptly of any personal data breach.
Persons authorized to process personal data must commit to confidentiality.
Documentation and Compliance:
Both Parties must demonstrate compliance with the Clauses.
The data exporter must provide necessary information to the data importer to demonstrate compliance and allow audits.
Data Subject Rights:
The data importer must handle data subject requests (e.g., access, rectification, erasure) promptly and free of charge.
Information to data subjects must be clear and accessible.
Use of Sub-processors:
Not applicable or not addressed in this amendment.
Non-compliance and Termination:
The data importer must inform the data exporter if unable to comply with the Clauses.
The data exporter may suspend data transfers or terminate the contract if compliance is not restored within a reasonable time.
Upon termination, personal data must be returned or deleted, with certification of deletion.
Governing Law and Jurisdiction:
The Clauses are governed by the law of Ireland.
Disputes are to be resolved by courts in Ireland or the EU Member State where the data subject resides.
Categories and Nature of Personal Data:
Covers a wide range of personal data types including identification data, contact details, employment data, health data, genetic data, and sensitive personal data.
Data subjects include employees, contractors, business partners, study team staff, and clinical trial participants.
Data processing includes collection, storage, use, disclosure, and destruction as necessary for service provision.
Technical and Organizational Measures (TOMs):
Access control to premises, systems, and data.
Disclosure control to secure data transfers.
Input control with audit trails.
Job control to ensure processing follows instructions.
Availability control to prevent data loss.
Segregation control to separate data for different purposes.
Breach Notification:
The data importer must notify the data exporter and supervisory authorities without undue delay in case of a personal data breach.
If the breach poses a high risk, affected data subjects must also be notified.
This amendment clearly strengthens and updates the data protection framework between Century Therapeutics and ICON Clinical Research Limited, ensuring GDPR compliance and robust safeguards for personal data involved in their services.
You can access the full Amendment 1 document here for more details:
Century Therapeutics November 2023 - Amendment # 1

Summary of Final Budget Reconciliation Provisions in IPSEN PHARMA S.A.S Master Services Agreement (MSA) as amended 28 August 2020
I found detailed information about the budget reconciliation and related financial provisions in the IPSEN PHARMA S.A.S Master Services Agreement, including its amendments as of 28 August 2020. Here are the key relevant points:
Budget Modifications and Amendments (Section 7.7):
Budgets are agreed based on key study parameters such as number of investigators, centers, subjects, countries, protocol design, and calendar.
Any changes to these key parameters may lead to amendments to the budget and/or calendar, which must be discussed in good faith.
The Service Provider cannot request budget increases caused by delays or defaults attributable to itself.
Budget modifications require formal amendment signatures unless otherwise agreed.
Additional Services and Budget Supplements (Section 7.8):
Additional services requested by IPSEN not covered in the original Work Order may result in budget supplements, subject to prior written agreement.
Final Reconciliation upon Early Termination (Section 7.9):
In case of early termination of a Work Order, a final reconciliation will be conducted after the full wind-down of services.
Invoice Disputes and Reconciliation (Section 7.10):
IPSEN must notify the Service Provider within 15 days of any invoice disputes.
Both parties will use good faith efforts to reconcile disputed amounts promptly.
Undisputed amounts must be paid according to terms.
Quarterly Budget Reconciliation (Section 7.11):
The Service Provider shall provide IPSEN with quarterly reconciliations of the study budget for direct fees, subcontractor fees, and pass-through expenses.
This reconciliation includes variances between contractual and actual amounts, reasons for variances (e.g., timing, delays), and exchange rate impacts.
Final Reconciliation after Study Completion (Section 7.9):
A final reconciliation is explicitly stated to occur after the full wind-down of services following early termination or study completion.
Additional Notes:
The agreement includes detailed schedules for budgets, invoices, and payment milestones.
The budget and reconciliation processes are designed to ensure transparency and good faith collaboration between IPSEN and the Service Provider.
The contract and amendments provide mechanisms for managing budget changes, disputes, and final financial settlements.
You can review the full contract document here for more details:
Ipsen Master Study Services Agreement - Document (28 Aug 2020)

results.expected_answer_excel_legacy:
## Retainer Provisions in Novo Nordisk MSA 292577 and Amendments

Based on the retrieved contract documents and amendments for Novo Nordisk A/S: MSA - Mar 21 (ID 292577), here is a summary of the retainer-related provisions:

### General Contract Context
- The MSA is active, executed on 13 April 2021, effective from 15 March 2021, and expires on 15 March 2027.
- The contract includes various provisions related to services, audits, regulatory compliance, insurance, and indemnification.
- The contract and amendments include detailed operational and compliance clauses but do not explicitly label a section as "retainer provisions."

### Relevant Provisions Related to Retainer Concepts
- **Non-Solicitation and Conversion Fees**: 
  - Novo Nordisk is allowed to solicit and hire Functional Service Provider (FSP) staff assigned under the Work Orders without a fee if the staff has provided services for at least 24 months. 
  - If Novo Nordisk hires FSP staff with less than 24 months of service, a conversion fee is payable to the Supplier Affiliate, calculated as a percentage of the staffâ€™s annual bill rate. 
  - The right to solicit and convert FSP staff is limited to 10% of all FSP staff performing functional services per calendar year unless otherwise approved in writing.

- **Payment and Invoicing**: 
  - Services are generally invoiced on a milestone basis following performance unless otherwise agreed in the applicable Work Order. 
  - Credit terms/payment days are 60 days.

- **Insurance and Liability**: 
  - The Supplier agrees to maintain insurance coverage including commercial general liability, professional liability, and umbrella liability with specified minimum limits. 
  - Novo Nordisk maintains clinical trial liability insurance and may self-insure.

- **Audit and Compliance**: 
  - Novo Nordisk has rights to audit the Supplierâ€™s records and compliance with the agreement. 
  - The Supplier must cooperate with audits and provide documentation as reasonably requested.

- **Data Protection and Reporting**: 
  - The Supplier must comply with data protection laws and provide information for transparency reporting related to payments to healthcare professionals (HCPs) and healthcare organizations (HCOs).

### Summary
- While there is no explicit "retainer" clause titled as such, the contract and amendments include provisions that govern ongoing service engagement, conversion fees for hiring staff, payment terms, and compliance obligations that collectively relate to the management of retained services.
- The conversion fee and non-solicitation clauses are the closest provisions related to retainer arrangements, specifying conditions under which Novo Nordisk may hire staff and associated fees.
- Payment terms and invoicing schedules are milestone-based with 60-day payment terms.

I’d be happy to confirm the MSA payment terms for Novo Nordisk, IPSEN, and Regeneron. Here’s what I found based on their respective MSAs and amendments:
Novo Nordisk A/S: MSA - Mar 21
Credit Terms: Payment due within 60 days.
Compensation Terms: Services invoiced on a milestone basis unless otherwise agreed in the Work Order. Rate cards with fixed rates through 31 Dec 2025, with inflation adjustments thereafter. Pass-through costs billed as incurred.
Commercial Terms: Liability cap is twice the fees paid under the relevant Work Order. Governing law is Denmark. Rebates and discounts apply. Change order provisions and indemnification clauses are included. GDPR-compliant Data Protection Addendum (DPA) is in place.
IPSEN: Ipsen-MSA_28 Aug 2020
Credit Terms: Payment due within 90 days.
Compensation Terms: Payments based on approved budgets in Work Orders. Rate card fixed for 3 years with inflation adjustments thereafter. Pass-through expenses billed monthly with no markup. Volume rebates and discounts apply.
Commercial Terms: Liability cap is the greater of €3,000,000 or 3x fees payable under the SOW, higher for personal data breaches. Governing law is England & Wales. Non-compete clause included. Indemnification and GDPR-compliant DPA are present.
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc: MSA - Nov 1 2021
Credit Terms: 90 days from invoice receipt for Direct Fees and Pass-Through Costs; 45 days for Investigator Fees.
Compensation Terms: Fees as per Work Orders; invoices in USD. Rate cards with inflation adjustments based on Mercer index. Investigator payments managed with quarterly forecasts and monthly reporting. Pass-through costs billed monthly. Volume discounts apply.
Commercial Terms: Liability cap is the greater of 3 times direct fees paid or $5 million; $8 million for data protection breaches. Governing law is New York. Penalty clauses, rebates, and volume discounts apply. Indemnification and GDPR-compliant DPA included.
